seoTitle: How to Populate a Word Template With Power Automate
metaDescription: Automatically fill Word document templates with dynamic data using Power Automate, from content controls to auto-generated files saved to OneDrive.
intro: Rebuilding contracts or invoices by hand every time is slow and error-prone. This guide explains how to set up a Word template with content controls and use Power Automate to populate it automatically with data from a cloud source, producing a ready-to-use document each time the flow runs.

steps.heading: Enable the Word Developer tab
steps.body: Turn on the Developer ribbon in Microsoft Word so you can access the content control tools needed to build a fillable template.

steps.heading: Insert content controls into your template
steps.body: Add plain text content controls wherever you want dynamic data to appear, and use repeating section controls for any tables that need multiple rows, such as invoice line items.

steps.heading: Upload the master template to OneDrive
steps.body: Save your finished template to OneDrive or SharePoint so Power Automate's Word connector can access it when the flow runs.

steps.heading: Build the Power Automate flow
steps.body: Create a cloud flow, fetch the records you want to merge into the document, and add the 'Populate a Microsoft Word Template' action pointing at your uploaded file.

steps.heading: Map your data to the template fields
steps.body: Match each content control tag to the corresponding field or array from your data source, including any repeating table rows.

steps.heading: Generate and save the output file
steps.body: Add a Create File action to save the newly populated document, then run a test to confirm the text and tables filled in correctly.

faq.question: Can I use this to automate contracts or invoices?
faq.answer: Yes, populating Word templates with Power Automate is commonly used for exactly that — generating contracts, invoices, and similar documents from a data source automatically.

faq.question: Does the Word template need to be stored in OneDrive?
faq.answer: It needs to be in a location Power Automate's Word Online connector can reach, and OneDrive or SharePoint are the standard supported storage options.

faq.question: What happens if my table has a variable number of rows?
faq.answer: This is what repeating section content controls are for — they let the template expand to fit however many rows your data array contains.

faq.question: Why isn't my data showing up in the generated document?
faq.answer: This is usually caused by a mismatch between the content control tag name in the template and the field name mapped in the Power Automate action, so double-check both match exactly.
